title: Blox AI /troubleshoot + /execute-action SSE event envelope
description: Phase 9 + Phase 10 + Phase 11 contract. Every event emitted by the Blox AI container's POST /troubleshoot AND POST /execute-action SSE streams MUST match exactly one of the variants below. The container is the primary enforcer (validates before emit); the fula-ota BLE proxy passes through but the executor additionally validates `recommended_action` and `/execute-action` requests against action_whitelist.json. Phase 10 added execution_result. Phase 11 adds session_started + user_question + user_reply_received.

tool.description: Must be one of the diag/* names registered in ble_commands.json. Phase 11 will add ai/user-reply etc.

payload.description: Tool-specific response. Tight typing (oneOf per tool) is deferred per Phase 9 advisor consensus — `any` keeps drift surface small while the container implementation settles.

action_name.description: Must be a key in action_whitelist.json tier_2_idempotent.actions or tier_3_destructive.actions. Phase 10 executor enforces (proxy does not).

approval_token.description: Phase 10 v2: bumped from 16/256 to 64/2048 to match execute_action_request.schema.json. Codex post-impl HIGH catch — a token sized 30 chars would validate on emit but get rejected at execute, breaking the contract.

session_started.description: Phase 11. ALWAYS the first event emitted on a new /troubleshoot SSE stream when no session_id was provided in the request. Client persists session_id and uses it for subsequent /troubleshoot/user-reply and /troubleshoot/phone-context calls. Dedicated event (not overloaded into `thought`) per Codex pre-impl HIGH — thought.payload is type:string and embedding {session_id} would break v2 render assumptions. `protocol_version` field added per post-impl review (Gemini HIGH, Codex MED): natural version-discovery point so client can branch logic before the first user_question lands.

protocol_version.description: SSE event envelope schema version. Matches `schema_version` field at the root of sse_events.schema.json. Bumped to 4 in any future SSE schema iteration so clients can negotiate.

ttl_seconds.description: How long the session stays alive without activity (sliding — refreshed on each /user-reply or /phone-context). Container's choice; typically 1800 (30 min) per plan.

user_question.description: Phase 11. Model asks the user a clarifying question. SSE stream PAUSES on the container side after emit until the client calls POST /troubleshoot/user-reply with matching question_id. App renders as chat bubble with input field (or pre-filled buttons if `options` present).

expected_response_type.description: Codex pre-impl HIGH renamed from expected_response_schema (which would imply embedded JSON Schema — too brittle for LLM emission). Discriminator tells the app which UI to render: text→input box, boolean→Yes/No buttons, choice→picker over `options`.

options.description: Pre-filled reply suggestions / choices. Required (≥2) when expected_response_type=choice; optional when text; should be absent when boolean (app uses Yes/No).

allOf.description: If expected_response_type=choice, options must be present + non-empty.
Inverse — when expected_response_type=boolean, options must NOT be present (the app renders Yes/No automatically; supplying options would create inconsistent UI). Built-in advisor post-impl catch.

user_reply_received.description: Phase 11. Container acknowledges that POST /troubleshoot/user-reply landed and the reply has been appended to the model's context. UX: app switches the user's reply bubble from 'sending...' to 'delivered'; model's subsequent `thought` events represent its response. Codex pre-impl MEDIUM said include session_id explicitly (rather than make client infer from local request state).

execution_result.description: Emitted by POST /execute-action after the executor finishes (success or failure). action_id matches the recommended_action that produced the approval_token. The shape is intentionally separate from `error` because tier-2/3 action failures are reportable runtime state, not an error in the SSE protocol itself.

stdout_excerpt.description: Truncate to <= 2048 bytes in the container (Gemini pre-impl HIGH: log-injection + disk-exhaustion mitigation).

follow_up.description: Optional next-step the model can read on its next turn (e.g. 'service restarted; re-run diag/summary to confirm').
